page: Vultr vbm-6c-32gb is a bare metal server belonging to the Bare Metal SSD family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 2286G processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.0 GHz, featuring 6 physical cores and 12 vCPUs via hyperthreading, supported by a 12 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding 2.67 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled with the server, consisting of a 1920 GB SSD. No GPU accelerators are present on this hardware configuration.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most standard compute workloads. The server achieves average ratings in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, alongside an average Passmark CPU score of 15132.19. Single-threaded gzip compression performs in the top tier at 35.51 GB/sec, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression is strong at 13.91 GB/sec. However, memory bandwidth presents a notable tradeoff; while small-block cached read bandwidth is average at 851.86 GB/sec, large-block uncached read and write memory bandwidths are weak, measuring 32.51 GB/sec and 14.71 GB/sec respectively. LLM inference performance is also weak for medium-sized 7B models, though it maintains average speeds for smaller 135M models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this bare metal instance provides dedicated hardware resources, high-capacity local SSD storage, and a high CPU clock speed without the overhead of virtualization. This resource density makes it suitable for workloads requiring consistent physical core access and substantial local storage. Appropriate workloads include static web serving, database operations, and general-purpose applications that do not require massive memory bandwidth or GPU acceleration.
description: Vultr vbm-6c-32gb is an x86_64 bare metal server in the Bare Metal SSD family, powered by an Intel Xeon 2286G processor with 6 physical cores, 12 vCPUs, and a 4.0 GHz clock speed. It features 32.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 1920 GB local SSD, with no GPU. Performance benchmarks show average CPU capabilities, highlighted by top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, a key tradeoff is its weak large-block uncached memory bandwidth. The dedicated hardware profile and high-capacity local storage make this server qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated physical resources. It is well-suited for static web serving, database operations, and general-purpose applications.

tagline: A dedicated bare metal server featuring high-frequency processing, substantial local SSD storage, and balanced general-purpose compute capabilities.

32.0 GB of system memory paired with a 1920 GB local SSD
Weak large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidth
Optimized for static web serving, database operations, and general-purpose workloads
